details: The scheme “Enhancement of Spoken English Programme” is implemented under the Samagra Shiksha Programme by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to enhance the spoken English skills of students of Classes 9 to 12 studying in all government schools across the districts of the state.
benefits: The programme was implemented for approximately 39,000 students of Classes 9 to 12 across 109 government schools in all districts of the state.
eligibility: The applicant must be a student of Classes 9 to 12 from one of the 109 selected government secondary schools in Uttarakhand.

offline: All students from the 109 selected schools, chosen based on factors such as internet connectivity, student strength, and availability of devices for online programmes.

documents_required: Documents required as per the guidelines of the respective school.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to enhance the spoken English skills of students of Classes 9 to 12 studying in government schools across Uttarakhand.

faqs.question: Who can participate in this programme?
faqs.answer: The programme is for students of Classes 9 to 12 from 109 selected government secondary schools in Uttarakhand.

faqs.question: How many students are benefitting from the programme?
faqs.answer: Approximately 39,000 students are participating in the programme.

faqs.question: How were the schools selected for the programme?
faqs.answer: Schools were selected based on internet connectivity, student strength, and availability of devices for online programmes.

faqs.question: Is the programme available in all districts of Uttarakhand?
faqs.answer: Yes, the programme is implemented in all districts of the state through selected government secondary schools.

faqs.question: What classes are covered under this programme?
